description: Follow-up from 2026-07-06 scan work. Public/HTTP bid scans ran, but protected portal scans cannot log in yet. Current state: GARDEN_BITWARDEN_CLI and GARDEN_BITWARDEN_CONFIG_DIR are configured; Vaultwarden service is running at https://garden.tail6f3b43.ts.net; the gnome_garden CLI profile is not logged in/unlocked; GARDEN_BITWARDEN_SESSION is empty/missing; SourceCredential resolution reports missing credentials for planetbids, bidnet, publicpurchase, opengov, and sam_gov. Work needed: log in/unlock the gnome_garden Bitwarden CLI profile, store a valid session in the service env and local ignored .env as appropriate, create or verify SourceCredential rows pointing at the correct Bitwarden item IDs/names, test credential resolution without printing secrets, then rerun a protected source scan. Also note related browser fallback issue from scan work: deployed release is missing agent-browser at /opt/gnome-garden/jido_browser-linux_amd64/agent-browser-linux-x64, causing :enoent on browser fallback scans.

comments.text: Committed code-side support in 5d38b38. Changes: release/deploy now installs and copies the jido_browser agent-browser bundle; Garden.Browser searches configured, release, build, and cwd binary roots before falling back; Bitwarden credential resolution now supports SAM.gov API keys from custom fields or login password; queued SAM.gov credential verification can resolve Bitwarden-backed credentials without persisting secrets; added focused tests for release browser lookup and Bitwarden-backed SAM.gov resolution/verification. Verification: PHX_SERVER=false PORT=4102 mix compile --warnings-as-errors passed; focused tests for browser/procurement credentials passed (25 tests). Remaining blocker: the gnome_garden Bitwarden CLI profile is still unauthenticated/locked and SourceCredential rows are missing locally, so protected portal scans still need operator credential setup with real Bitwarden item IDs/names before the issue can be closed.

Operational follow-up: created local non-secret family-level SourceCredential rows for planetbids, publicpurchase, opengov, bidnet, and sam_gov using the UI default Bitwarden item names (PlanetBids, PublicPurchase, OpenGov, BidNet, SAM.gov). Verified app status after creation: each family reports app_status=pending and configured?=false, so scans are not falsely unblocked. Remaining blocker is still Bitwarden CLI authentication/session plus verification against real vault items.

Committed fd1bc94 to treat the dev-shell PGPORT edit as part of this Garden work. flake.nix now defaults local Postgres to 5433 while preserving .env/environment PGPORT overrides; verified nix develop reports the configured project PGPORT=5434 and git diff --check is clean.

Completed protected BidNet path in production. Commits since last breadcrumb: efd2a99 verifies Bitwarden-backed source credentials directly, 73ab216/2ed6d91/5c3f819 make the Playwright runner work from releases without shell/stdin option issues, 1449a73 packages Playwright runtime deps into the release, 2360e20 uses a browser-like Playwright context for BidNet, and e15c0a7 uses browser headers for BidNet HTTP scans. Operational changes: service env has BW_SESSION/GARDEN_BITWARDEN_SESSION, /opt/gnome-garden-shared is writable by the service via NixOS ReadWritePaths, team-vault Bitwarden refs are configured for BidNet and eCenterDirect, and BidNet/eCenterDirect sources were created. Production verification on 2026-07-06: BidNet SCADA session refresh succeeded with valid session e76f3d58-fd84-4117-b486-5148bb2bb25e and storage state; direct ListingScanner scan for source 33f27b0c-2ab5-443d-bac4-a1cf56eba66f extracted 9, scored 9, saved 9 with diagnosis saved_qualified_leads. Service is active. Follow-up remains for additional provider-specific adapters/credentials beyond the two team-vault items.

title: Enable Bitwarden-backed authenticated procurement scans
